person.name: Graaf Arnold IV van Loon
person.alternateName: Arnold IV van Loon en Chiny

memoryArtifacts.headline: vrijheidscharter mei 1232
memoryArtifacts.description:
In naam van de Heilige en onverdeelde Drievuldigheid. Arnold, graaf van Loon, aan allen die dit schrift zullen lezen heil in eeuwigheid.

Meestal pleegt er betwisting te ontstaan over de menselijke handelingen, tenzij ze door gezegelde brieven vereeuwigd zijn. Het weze dus bekend aan onze generatie en aan de toekomende, dat ik Arnold, graaf van Loon, hetzelfde recht en dezelfde vrijheid, die de bisschopsstad Luik bezit, in alles en over alles, zowel van rechtswege als door de gewoonte, aan mijn villa Hasselt heb geschonken, om ze voor eeuwig te genieten, vermits zij datzelfde recht en diezelfde vrijheid van mijn voorgangers gekregen heeft, behoudens nochtans voor mij en mijn nakomelingen, de molens, brouwerijen, tollen en mijn andere inkomsten reeds geïnd of nog te innen. Opdat echter deze vrijheid volkomen en ongeschonden voortschrijde, heb ik ze samen met de edelen en notabelen van mijn land bevestigd en heb ik dit getuigschrift door het aanhangen van mijn zegel bekrachtigd.

Gedaan en gegeven plechtig in de maand mei in het jaar 's Heren geboorte 1232.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Graaf Arnold IV van Loon於大約1203年出生在比利時利包格波格洛時，他的父親Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz29歲，母親Kunigunde von Zimmern24歲。他於1224年與Jeanne de Chiny結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1273年2月22日在海因里希_海普洛文次可萊斯 斯享年71歲，埋葬在荷蘭努-拉藍德貝肯洛德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Graaf Arnold IV van Loon于大约1203年出生在比利时利包格波格洛时，他的父亲Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz29岁，母亲Kunigunde von Zimmern24岁。他于1224年与Jeanne de Chiny结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1273年2月22日在海因里希_海普洛文次可萊斯 斯享年71岁，埋葬在荷兰努-拉藍德貝肯洛德。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Loon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

variant of Lond, from Middle English launde ‘glade’, or from one of the placenames derived from it such as Laund (of which there are examples in Yorkshire, Lancashire, and Leicestershire), or from the abbey of this name in Leicestershire. Compare Lon .
possibly a variant of English Lund , which is either a topographic name from Old Norse lundr ‘grove’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word.

variants.name: Lon
meanings: English: probably a variant of Lond, from Middle English launde ‘glade’, or from one of the placenames derived from it such as Laund (of which there are examples in Yorkshire, Lancashire, and Leicestershire), or from the abbey of this name in Leicestershire. Compare Lown .
Cambodian: written លន់, corresponding to the Khmer word meaning e.g. ‘(to be) superb’. Compare   Lun   4.

Burmese (Shan): from a personal name usually forming part of a compound name, of unexplained etymology. Compare   Lone   4. — Note: Since Shans do not have hereditary surnames, this name was registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.

variants.name: Lowndes
meanings: English: variant of   Lown  .

variants.name: Lownes
meanings: English: variant of   Lown  .

variants.name: Lundy
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: habitational name from any of several places called Lundie, for example one near Doune in Perthshire.
Irish: topographic name from Anglo-Norman French de la Launde ‘of the glade’. Compare Lown 1.
Americanized form of Norwegian   Lunde  .

nameMeanings.name: Arnold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: 1  Jewish: Yiddish contracted form of   Aaron .
2  English: short form of   Arnold .

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Vivian   and   Vivien .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Graaf Arnold IV van Loon was born about 1203, in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgium, his father, Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz, was 29 and his mother, Kunigunde von Zimmern, was 24. He married Jeanne de Chiny in 1224. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 22 February 1273, in Neuss, Rhineland, Prussia, at the age of 71, and was buried in Berkenrode, North Holland, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Graaf Arnold IV van Loon est né environ 1203, à Borgloon, Limbourg, Belgique, son père, Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Kunigunde von Zimmern, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Jeanne de Chiny en 1224. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 22 février 1273, à Kreis Neuss, Rhénanie, Prusse, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterré à Berkenrode, Hollande-septentrionale, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Graaf Arnold IV van Loon um 1203 in Borgloon, Limburg, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz 29 und seine Mutter Kunigunde von Zimmern 24. Er hat 1224 Jeanne de Chiny geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 22. Februar 1273 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Kreis Neuss, Rheinprovinz, Preußen und wurde in Berkenrode, Nord-Holland, Niederlande begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Graaf Arnold IV van Loon è nato il circa 1203 nel Borgloon, Limburgo, Belgio, il suo padre, Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Kunigunde von Zimmern, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Jeanne de Chiny nel 1224. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 22 febbraio 1273, nel Kreis Neuss, Rheinprovinz, Preußen, all'età di 71 anni, e fu sepolto a Berkenrode, Noord-Holland, Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Graaf Arnold IV van Loonが約1203年にベルギーリンブルグボルグロオンで生まれた時，父Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainzは29歳，母Kunigunde von Zimmernは24歳であった。1224年にJeanne de Chinyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼は1273年2月22日にプレウssンルヘインプロヴィンズクレイス ネウスで71歳で死去し，オランダノオルド-hオランドバーケンロドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Graaf Arnold IV van Loon(이)가 대략 1203년 벨기에 림버그 볼그루에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Kunigunde von Zimmern(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1224년 Jeanne de Chiny(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1273년 2월 22일 플레우ss 리인플로빈츠 크레이스 니우스에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 네덜란드 노르드-홀랜드 버켄로드에 묻혔다.

pt.Berkenrode, , North Holland, Netherlands: Berkenrode, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Graaf Arnold IV van Loon nasceu aproximadamente 1203, em Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica, seu pai, Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Kunigunde von Zimmern, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Jeanne de Chiny em 1224. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 22 de fevereiro de 1273, em Kreis Neuss, Rheinprovinz, Preußen, com 71 anos, e foi sepultado em Berkenrode, Holanda do Norte, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Graaf Arnold IV van Loon родился около 1203 г. в местности борглун, лимбург, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Kunigunde von Zimmern, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Jeanne de Chiny в 1224 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 22 февраля 1273 г. в местности крис неусс, раинпровинз, Пруссия в возрасте 71 год и был похоронен в местности биркенрод, нурд-холланд, Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Graaf Arnold IV van Loon nació aproximadamente 1203, en Borgloon, Limburgo, Bélgica, su papá, Gerard III van Loon Rieneck Mainz, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Kunigunde von Zimmern, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Jeanne de Chiny en 1224. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 22 de febrero de 1273, en Kreis Neuss, Rheinprovinz, Preußen, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultado en Berkenrode, Noord-Holland, Países Bajos.
